hypothesis: Gary Antonacci's Dual Momentum combines absolute momentum (time-series) and relative momentum (cross-sectional) to generate risk-adjusted returns. The absolute momentum filter (asset return vs. cash/T-bills) acts as a bear market hedge — go to cash when an asset is trending down absolutely. Relative momentum selects the best-performing asset from a universe. Applied to ETFs, this exploits documented momentum persistence (12-month lookback) while avoiding the worst drawdowns.
universe: ETFs: SPY (US equity), QQQ (Nasdaq/tech), IWM (small-cap), DIA (Dow), XLF (financials), XLE (energy), XLK (tech), XLV (healthcare)

prior_attempt_iter3.failure_mode: Too few trades (23.5 < 50 minimum). Likely monthly rebalance with small universe. Need more frequent rebalance OR larger universe OR lower threshold.

variants_to_explore.name: dual_momentum_weekly_rebalance
variants_to_explore.description: Weekly rebalance instead of monthly — generates 4x more signals, increases trade count dramatically

variants_to_explore.name: dual_momentum_short_lookback
variants_to_explore.description: Shorter lookback (21-42 days / 1-2 months) for more responsive signals
variants_to_explore.expected_benefit: More frequent turnover, captures shorter momentum cycles

variants_to_explore.name: dual_momentum_multi_asset
variants_to_explore.description: Expand universe to 12+ ETFs including GLD, TLT, EEM — more selection candidates
variants_to_explore.expected_benefit: Hold top 2 ETFs simultaneously — doubles average positions, more trades

variants_to_explore.name: dual_momentum_top_n_portfolio
variants_to_explore.description: Hold top N assets by momentum (N=2 or 3) instead of single winner-take-all
variants_to_explore.expected_benefit: Diversified momentum portfolio, more trades, lower drawdown

variants_to_explore.name: dual_momentum_ma_filter
variants_to_explore.description: Add 200-day MA trend filter: only hold if price > 200d MA (absolute momentum proxy via price level)
variants_to_explore.expected_benefit: Regime filter reduces drawdown during bear markets, especially 2022

variants_to_explore.name: dual_momentum_vol_scaled
variants_to_explore.description: Scale position size by inverse 20-day realized volatility for each ETF held
variants_to_explore.expected_benefit: Equalizes risk contribution per holding — better risk-adjusted returns

variants_to_explore.name: dual_momentum_sector_rotation
variants_to_explore.description: Pure cross-sectional momentum on sector ETFs (XLF, XLE, XLK, XLV, XLI, XLC), hold top 2
variants_to_explore.expected_benefit: Sector rotation within S&P 500 — low correlation to broad market, documented alpha

implementation_notes.vectorbt_approach: Use vbt.Portfolio.from_signals() with daily data. Rebalance signals on every Nth day using modulo on index. For top-N selection, set entries=True for top N tickers by rolling return, exits=True for tickers dropping out of top N.
implementation_notes.absolute_momentum_filter: Compare each ETF's N-day return to 0 (or to a risk-free proxy). If return < 0, exclude from selection — go to cash for that allocation.
implementation_notes.relative_momentum: Rank ETFs by lookback_days total return. Hold top_n with highest positive return.
implementation_notes.rebalance_signal_generation: Create a boolean DataFrame entries where on every rebalance_day, set True for selected tickers, False for others. Set exits as the inverse — exit non-selected.
implementation_notes.trade_count_target: Must achieve >= 50 IS trades. Weekly rebalance on 8 ETFs holding top 2 = ~(52 weeks * 2 assets) = ~100 trades/year. 5-year IS period = ~500 trades easily.
implementation_notes.drawdown_target: IS max drawdown must be < 20%. Absolute momentum filter (cash when trending down) is critical for 2022.

known_failure_modes: Monthly rebalance with small universe generates too few trades — fails 50-trade minimum
Momentum crashes: sharp reversals (2020-03, 2022) cause loss if absolute momentum filter absent
Winner-take-all (top 1 only) concentrates risk — prefer top 2 or 3
Long lookbacks (252 days) are too slow to react to regime shifts
Survivorship bias: using current ETF universe only, not historical constituents

historical_performance.notes: Key: use weekly rebalance + top 2-3 holdings + absolute momentum filter. These three changes transform the strategy from a few dozen trades to hundreds, dramatically improving statistical significance and trade count requirements.

references: AQR - Fact, Fiction and Momentum Investing
